campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 19, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council held a meeting on May 19, 2026, where they discussed staffing vacancies and efforts to recruit and retain city employees. The council also addressed an upcoming Boogie Festival happening that weekend.
keyTopics: City staffing vacancies and recruitment/retention strategies
Public hearing on government code section 3502 matters
Boogie Festival event this weekend

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 6, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council approved preliminary engineering reports and took steps toward a tax measure, including scheduling a public hearing and planning an ordinance vote for May 20. The council also discussed whether Saratoga should become a charter city and reviewed potential tax options to understand revenue possibilities.
keyTopics: Engineer's Report preliminary approval for fiscal year
Public hearing scheduled (details on measure to be determined)
Ordinance vote planned for May 20, 2026
Charter city status discussion and staff direction
Preliminary tax options and revenue estimates presented

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — June 2, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council held a meeting on June 2, 2026, to approve routine administrative items and set up an election for later that year. Key decisions included confirming a new youth commissioner, finalizing a housing development map, and approving multi-year contracts with software and services vendors.
keyTopics: November 2026 municipal election scheduled for three positions
New youth commissioner appointment confirmed by selection committee
Final approval of Tract 10675 housing development map on Boulevard
Five-year software agreement with Accela, Inc. authorized
Budget authorization for revenue and expenditures

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 2, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met on June 2, 2026, to approve routine meeting minutes and handle business for two local commercial districts. The council addressed budget and funding plans for the Alum Rock Santa Clara Street and Alameda Business Improvement Districts for the upcoming fiscal year.
keyTopics: Alum Rock Santa Clara Street Business Improvement District 2026-2027 budget and assessments
Alameda Business Improvement District 2026-2027 budget and assessments
Public hearing scheduled on assessment levies for both districts

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 2, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on June 2, 2026 that allowed residents to participate either in person or online via Zoom. The meeting included a public comment period where residents could raise their hands to speak on agenda items.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom for residents unable to attend in person
Public comment procedures for both online and phone participants

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 2, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held two back-to-back special meetings on June 2, 2026. The first was a closed-door session (details not disclosed to the public), followed by an evening special meeting with items on a special order of the day agenda.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting held (specific topics not publicly listed)
Special order of the day items discussed at evening meeting
City Council convened for special meetings rather than regular session

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — June 2, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to approve proclamations celebrating LGBTQ+ Pride Month in June and recognizing the UN's World Environment Day on June 5th. The council also opened the floor for residents to speak about any topics within the city's authority that weren't on the official agenda.
keyTopics: LGBTQ+ Pride Month proclamation for June
United Nations World Environment Day recognition (June 5)
Public comment period for non-agenda items (30-minute limit)

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — June 1, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's City Council is holding Stadium Neighborhood Relations Ad Hoc Subcommittee meetings to discuss issues affecting the stadium neighborhood. Residents can attend in person or join remotely via Zoom, and can submit written comments by email before each meeting.
keyTopics: Stadium neighborhood relations and community concerns
In-person and remote participation options for residents
Written public comment process (email deadline: 10 a.m. one day before meeting)

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — June 2, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve the city's spending plan for fiscal year 2026-27 and handle routine administrative matters like approving meeting minutes and setting the calendar for upcoming meetings. The council also discussed changes to how city commissions schedule their appointment cycles.
keyTopics: Approved the 2026-27 operating budget for the city
Updated the commission appointment process to use quarterly cycles
Reviewed upcoming agenda items requested by council members

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 27, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to review the city's five-year capital improvement plan (2027-2031) and hear updates on housing and parking initiatives. The council also discussed a new prohousing designation program and downtown parking modernization efforts.
keyTopics: 2027-2031 Capital Improvement Plan review and recommendations
Prohousing Designation Program application materials
Downtown Parking Modernization Initiatives
Comprehensive Plan compliance
